title: General Information about the Student Council of IT STEP University
text: The Student Council of IT STEP University is a student self-government body that represents the interests of the university's student community, promotes the development of student life, organizes educational, cultural, social, and charitable initiatives, and ensures effective communication between students, university administration, and external partners. The Student Council is a key element in forming an active student community that not only participates in the educational process but also actively engages in public activities, volunteering, developing the university environment, and implementing student initiatives. The main goal of the Student Council's activities is to create conditions for the development of students as active, responsible, and conscious citizens who are able to take responsibility, initiate changes, and influence the development of the university community.

title: General Information about the Student Council of IT STEP University
text: The official name of the student self-government body is the Student Council of IT STEP University. This name is used in all official documents, communications, presentations, and external interactions. The abbreviated form is the Student Council of IT STEP University. In internal communication, the term Student Council may also be used.

title: General Information about the Student Council of IT STEP University
topic: Role of the Student Council in the University
text: The Student Council plays an important role in the functioning of the university community. It acts as a representative body for students and provides them with the opportunity to participate in shaping university life. The main roles of the Student Council include representation, organization, communication, and social responsibility. It represents student interests before the administration, teaching staff, external partners, city authorities, and other organizations. It organizes cultural, educational, sports, charitable, and volunteer events. It ensures continuous communication between students and administration to raise issues, propose ideas, and share information. Furthermore, it fosters social responsibility by organizing charity collections, donor campaigns, volunteer projects, and support for the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

title: Main Activities of the Student Council
topic: Student Events, Charity, and Community Development
text: The Student Council's activities cover several key areas. It organizes large-scale student events such as Andriyivski Vechornytsi, Student's Day, MindGames, sports tournaments, educational lectures, workshops, and guest meetings, which foster student culture. It also actively participates in charity and volunteering, organizing fundraisers, blood donation drives, support for the Armed Forces of Ukraine, and social initiatives. Furthermore, the council develops the student community by providing opportunities to show leadership, implement ideas, organize events, and gain management and communication skills.

title: Physical Location of Activities
text: The main location for the Student Council's activities is the Student Space, located in Room 102 of IT STEP University. This space is utilized for team meetings, event organization, initiative planning, working on student projects, and communicating with students.

title: The Role of the Student Council in Shaping University Culture
text: The Student Council plays a vital role in shaping the university's student culture. Through various initiatives, it builds an active student community, establishes university traditions, and fosters a culture of mutual assistance, leadership, and responsible citizenship. It is through the student council that many students gain their first experience in organizational activities, project management, and teamwork.

title: General Development of Student Self-Government
text: The IT STEP University Student Council is a student self-government body that formed gradually alongside the development of the university's student community. Active and systematic operations began around 2022, and since then the council has grown into an organized team engaged in cultural, educational, and charitable projects.

title: 2022–2023 Period (Head — Olha Zadolynna)
text: During this period, the foundations of active student engagement were laid and a core team was formed. A major achievement was joining the Lviv Student Council, which opened new opportunities for cooperation with other universities in the city and participation in municipal projects.

title: 2023–2024 Period (Head — Dzvenyslava Yankiv)
text: This stage was marked by further development and structuring of the organization. A key event was joining the Ukrainian Association of Students (UAS), which allowed the council to represent students' interests at the national level and participate in shaping youth policy.

title: Period from 2024 (Head — Markiyan Kostur)
text: During this term, the council's activities expanded significantly. Numerous fundraisers were organized to support the Armed Forces of Ukraine (raising over 250,000 UAH), alongside large-scale blood donation drives involving over 200 students, setting a record for student blood donation in Lviv.

title: Development of Student Traditions: Andriyivski Vechornytsi and MindGames
text: Andriyivski Vechornytsi is the largest cultural event (gathering 100–120 participants) that combines Ukrainian traditions with charity. MindGames are intellectual games designed for university students and partners, promoting logical thinking and teamwork.

title: Sports Activities
text: The Student Council actively develops sports initiatives. An IT STEP University football team was created to compete in local tournaments, and volleyball matches alongside other sports competitions are regularly organized for the students.

title: Significance of Historical Development
text: The Student Council is a vital part of the university ecosystem, uniting active students and creating opportunities to realize new initiatives. It improves student life and helps form a new generation of leaders ready to take responsibility.

title: General Organizational Structure
text: The IT STEP University Student Council has a flexible organizational structure based on the distribution of responsibilities among team members and various departments. Key elements include the head, deputy heads, council members, functional departments, and project teams.

title: Head of the Student Council
text: The Head of the Student Council coordinates the overall activities of the student self-government. Currently headed by Markiyan Kostur, this role involves strategic planning, representing student interests before the university administration, initiating new projects, and interacting with external partners and student organizations.

title: Deputy Heads of the Student Council
text: Deputy heads assist in coordinating team activities and managing specific departments. For the 2025-2026 academic year, the deputies are Anna Romanyk, Liliia Labetska, and Illia Bredikhin. Notable former deputies include Oleksandr Serediak (currently the Student Vice-Mayor of Lviv) and Anastasiia Sheianova, who both significantly contributed to the development of student self-government.

title: Members of the Student Council
text: The council consists of active university students engaged in organizing events and implementing projects. The number of members varies by term, with approximately 20 members in the 2025-2026 academic year, 14 in 2024-2025, and 18 in 2023-2024.

title: SMM and Communication Department
text: This department manages information activities and student communication, primarily through the @its_studrada Instagram page. Key members include Maiia Tsekhosh (SMM manager), Yulianna Priadka (content creation and video editing), and Marta Snitynska (designer responsible for visual materials and merch design).

title: Technical Initiatives Department
text: This department focuses on implementing technical solutions and innovations. Members like Pavlo Furhalo, Adam-Petro Ohorodnyk, and Roman Sukhai assist with technical support, event organization, and the realization of technical projects.

title: Merch Development Department
text: A new direction focused on university merchandise. Activities include merch design, website development for sales, communication with manufacturers, and sales organization. Team members involved are Solomiia Bilyk, Daryna Hrydko, and Marta Snitynska.

title: Organizational Department
text: Responsible for the preparation and execution of student events. Members such as Sofiia Shabatura, Andrii Bida, Zlata Pelishko, Stefaniia Koldovska, and Oleksandra help coordinate participants and manage logistics during events.

title: Structural Flexibility
text: The structure of the Student Council is not rigidly fixed. It adapts based on the number of team members, new initiatives, and the evolving needs of the student community, allowing the organization to respond quickly to new challenges.

title: General Principle of Team Formation
text: The IT STEP University Student Council is an open organization for students willing to develop student life, organize events, and represent the community. Joining is voluntary, requiring an active lifestyle and an interest in public activities. The council seeks students with diverse skills to form a multifunctional team.

title: Attracting Students at the Beginning of the Academic Year
text: Most new members join at the start of the academic year. The council team visits first-year students during their initial study days to present their activities, explain the role of student self-government, showcase major events, and invite freshmen to join.

title: Alternative Ways to Join
text: Besides freshman presentations, students can join by writing to the head of the Student Council, contacting any council member, messaging the official Instagram page, or reaching out to representatives personally. The council is always open to new active participants.

title: Interviewing Candidates
text: After expressing interest, candidates undergo a short interview. The goal is to get to know the student, understand their motivation, identify their strengths, and determine the best area of activity for them. Discussions cover the student's ideas, previous experience, and skills they want to develop.

title: Distribution by Areas of Activity
text: Once accepted, new members are assigned to various areas based on their interests and skills. These areas include event organization, SMM and communications, content creation, design, technical initiatives, merch development, volunteering projects, and external events.

title: Role of New Members
text: New members typically start by helping organize events and participating in smaller initiatives. This helps them integrate into the team, understand the council's workflow, gain event-planning experience, and develop communication skills before taking on more responsibility in larger projects.

title: Importance of Participation
text: Participating in the Student Council provides valuable experience for future careers. It allows students to develop leadership, teamwork, communication, and project management skills while engaging in socially significant initiatives, serving as a platform for forming future leaders.

title: General Role of Student Events
text: Organizing events is a key activity of the IT STEP University Student Council. These events help build an active student environment, foster integration, and improve communication across different years. The council organizes a variety of cultural, charitable, educational, and sports events, many of which have become annual traditions.

title: Andriyivski Vechornytsi
text: Held in December, this is one of the largest cultural and charitable events, gathering 100-120 participants. It combines traditional Ukrainian ceremonies, interactive activities, and informal networking with fundraising campaigns to support the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

title: Blood Donation Drives
text: The Student Council actively collaborates with medical institutions to organize blood donation drives. These initiatives have involved over 200 student donors, saved more than 600 lives, and set a student donation record in the Lviv region, greatly promoting social responsibility.

title: MindGames
text: MindGames are intellectual team games designed for students, university partners, and IT industry representatives. These unique events aim to develop logical thinking, enhance teamwork, and build a culture of healthy intellectual competition.

title: Student's Day
text: Every November, the Student Council organizes celebrations for International Students' Day. The events feature entertainment, interactive games, student performances, and team competitions, creating a festive atmosphere that unites students from all years.

title: Sports Events
text: The council actively supports sports initiatives, highlighted by the creation of the IT STEP University football team, which competes against other Lviv universities and schools. They also regularly organize volleyball tournaments and other sports activities.

title: Educational Events
text: The Student Council hosts various educational events, including lectures, guest meetings, and panel discussions. These feature IT industry representatives, experts, and university partners, giving students the chance to gain new knowledge and network with professionals.

title: Student Trips
text: To foster a friendly community, the council organizes trips, including popular mountain retreats during autumn, winter, or summer. These trips focus on team building, active recreation, and informal networking. The council also assists the university administration with organizing trips abroad.

title: Significance of Student Events
text: Organizing events is crucial for shaping the university's student culture. These activities unite students, establish university traditions, provide opportunities for self-realization, and make campus life more active, interesting, and diverse.

title: General Principles of Electing the Head of the Student Council
text: The Head of the IT STEP University Student Council is elected democratically, ensuring transparency and allowing students to directly influence the student community's development. All eligible university students have the right to vote to update or confirm the council's leadership.

title: Nomination of Candidates
text: The election process begins with candidate nominations. Any university student who wants to develop student self-government, has a clear vision, is ready to take responsibility, and has a supporting team can submit their candidacy and prepare an election program.

title: Election Campaign
text: During the campaign, candidates present their vision, ideas for student life, plans for new projects, and their intended team. This period allows students to understand each candidate's program through presentations and meetings before making a voting decision.

title: Presentation of Candidate Programs
text: A key stage of the election process is the presentation of programs. Candidates share their experience, future student projects, and the team they plan to form. This allows students to evaluate the proposed direction for the Student Council's development.

title: Voting
text: After the campaign, voting takes place involving all eligible university students. The candidate who receives the highest student support becomes the new Head of the Student Council, ensuring direct student influence on leadership formation.

title: Term of Office
text: The Head of the Student Council is elected for a one-year term. During this period, they are responsible for organizing council activities, coordinating the team, implementing initiatives, representing students before the administration, and developing projects.

title: Possibility of Extending the Term
text: If no new candidates run in the next election, the current Head can extend their term. This requires an accountability conference where the Head reports on the previous year's results and presents future plans. With support from students and the administration, the term can be extended.

title: Role of the Head of the Student Council
text: The Head is a key figure in student self-government. Main functions include coordinating council activities, organizing the team, representing students before the university administration and at external events, and acting as the primary liaison between students and the administration.

title: General Information about the Composition of the Student Council
text: The IT STEP University Student Council consists of active students who develop student life, organize events, and represent the community. Member counts vary by academic year: approximately 18 in 2023-2024, 14 in 2024-2025, and 20 in 2025-2026.

title: Head of the Student Council
text: Markiyan Kostur is currently serving his second term as the Head of the Student Council. He is responsible for coordinating the council's activities, organizing the team, implementing initiatives, and representing students before the university administration and at external events.

title: Deputy Heads of the Student Council (2025-2026)
text: The current deputies are Anna Romanyk (who coordinates the team and manages text content such as social media posts and event announcements), Liliia Labetska (who develops initiatives and organizes events), and Illia Bredikhin (who develops projects and supports council activities).

title: Former Deputy Heads of the Student Council
text: Former deputy Oleksandr Serediak currently serves as an advisor and the Student Vice-Mayor of Lviv, representing the council at the city level. Anastasiia Sheianova, another former deputy, continues to actively participate in realizing internal projects and university events.

title: Visual Communication and SMM Team
text: Marta Snitynska is the designer handling all visual communication, including social media posts, event posters, and merch design. Maiia Tsekhosh manages SMM, running the Instagram page and communicating with students. Yulianna Priadka creates and edits video content for student events and social media.

title: Technical Initiatives and Merch Team
text: Solomiia Bilyk and Daryna Hrydko work on university merch development and the related website. Stefaniia Koldovska, Pavlo Furhalo, Adam-Petro Ohorodnyk (who frequently hosts events), and Roman Sukhai handle technical issues, consult the team, and implement technological solutions for events.

title: Organizational Team
text: Zlata Pelishko, Sofiia Shabatura, Andrii Bida, and Oleksandra actively assist with organizing student events, design support, and the realization of various student initiatives.

title: Cooperation with Other Organizations
text: The Student Council actively collaborates with partners like the Ukrainian Association of Students (joined during Dzvenyslava Yankiv's term) and the Lviv Student Council (joined in 2022). This cooperation allows students to participate in city events, student forums, and inter-university projects.

title: Role of the Student Council in the University
text: The Student Council plays a vital role in developing student life. Its main functions include representing student interests before the administration, organizing events, supporting charity projects, fostering self-government, and creating an active, interesting, and diverse student environment.

title: Formation of Student Traditions
text: Traditions play a crucial role in developing the university's student environment. The IT STEP University Student Council actively creates and maintains traditions that unite students across different years, foster communication, and build an active student community.

title: Initiation of Freshmen
text: The initiation of freshmen features two main formats. The official part is a student ID presentation ceremony held in September. The informal initiation involves a mountain trip in September or October, aimed at integrating freshmen into the community, networking, and team building.

title: Andriyivski Vechornytsi
text: Launched in 2024 and held for the second time in 2025, this cultural and charitable event combines Ukrainian folk traditions, interactive activities, and student games. Gathering around 100-120 students, it includes a charity fundraiser to support the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

title: MindGames
text: MindGames are team-based intellectual competitions focused on developing logical thinking, teamwork, and analytical skills. Held approximately once a year, often around Student's Day, these games engage university students, partners, and IT company representatives.

title: Sports Traditions
text: Sports activities are becoming a vital university tradition. In 2025, the IT STEP University football team was established to compete in local university and school tournaments. The Student Council also organizes other sports events, such as volleyball tournaments.

title: Charitable Initiatives
text: Volunteering and charity are significant traditions. A major initiative is the blood donation drive, which engaged over 200 student donors, saved over 600 lives, and set a student donation record in the Lviv region, fostering a culture of social responsibility.

title: Significance of Traditions for the University
text: Council traditions are essential for shaping an active student community. They help students from different years connect, develop the university's student culture, and create opportunities for self-realization, with the council continuously working on establishing new traditions.

title: Role of Volunteering
text: Volunteering is a vital component of the Student Council's activities. It aims to develop a culture of mutual aid, engage students in socially significant initiatives, build a responsible civil society, and support charitable projects to help those in need.

title: Blood Donation Drives
text: Organized in collaboration with medical institutions, blood donation drives are a major volunteer initiative. By promoting this culture among youth, over 200 students have become donors, saving more than 600 lives and significantly assisting medical facilities.

title: Donation Record
text: During one of the drives, the Student Council set a student blood donation record in the Lviv region with over 80 individual donations. This achievement highlights the active readiness of IT STEP University students to help others.

title: Charity Fundraisers for the Armed Forces of Ukraine
text: The council frequently organizes charity fundraisers to support the Armed Forces of Ukraine. These collections take place during various student, charitable, and cultural events, such as the Andriyivski Vechornytsi, allowing students to directly assist the defenders of Ukraine.

title: Student Participation in Volunteering Initiatives
text: The Student Council actively encourages students to join donation drives, fundraisers, and social initiatives. This participation not only helps those in need but also fosters social responsibility and an active civic stance among the youth.

title: Significance of Volunteering Activity
text: Volunteering is crucial for the development of the student community. It allows students to contribute to society, forms a culture of mutual aid, and provides practical experience in charity projects, showcasing how students can unite for a greater cause.

title: General Role of External Representation
text: The IT STEP University Student Council actively represents the university at various external events, forums, student meetings, and inter-university initiatives. This activity is crucial for developing student self-government, networking with other organizations, exchanging experiences, and contributing to the city's and country's student environment.

title: Cooperation with the Lviv Student Council
text: Joining the Lviv Student Council in 2022 allowed the university's Student Council to participate in city-level student events, represent university interests, collaborate with other councils, and engage in joint student projects across Lviv.

title: Representation in Lviv Student Self-Government
text: A key element of university representation is participation in the city's student self-government structure. For instance, former deputy head Oleksandr Serediak currently serves as the Student Vice-Mayor of Lviv, giving the IT STEP University Student Council a strong voice in the local student community.

title: Cooperation with the Ukrainian Association of Students
text: The Student Council is a member of the Ukrainian Association of Students (UAS), having joined during Dzvenyslava Yankiv's term. Participation in this national organization enables the council to engage in national initiatives, collaborate with universities nationwide, attend forums, and exchange self-government experiences.

title: Participation in Student Events and Forums
text: Council representatives regularly attend various external events organized by other universities or public initiatives, including forums, conferences, educational events, and panel discussions. This helps students expand their networks and contribute to the broader student movement.

title: University Representation
text: By participating in external events, representatives boost IT STEP University's visibility, build partnerships with other student organizations, and develop inter-university cooperation, making the university an active participant in Lviv's student environment.

title: Significance of External Activity
text: External activities are vital for the development of student self-government. They help advance student initiatives, establish inter-university cooperation, and represent students' interests while contributing to the broader student community of the city and the country.
